channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Four Nacon-owned studios file for insolvency – Spiders, Cyanide, Kylotonn and Nacon Tech
item.content:encoded:
Four Nacon subsidiary studios have filed for insolvency earlier this week, as the French publisher tries to dig its way out of financial crisis.

As of Monday 23rd March, game developers Spiders (GreedFall, Steelrising), Cyanide (Styx, Blood Bowl, Tour de France), Kylotonn (Test Drive Unlimited Solar Crown, WRC) and motion capture company Nacon Tech have all filed for insolvency separately from their parent company. This allows them to individually partake in judicial reorganisation proceedings and find a way back towards sustainability.

As for Nacon themselves, they have “begun work on the outline of a restructuring plan for the group comprising the Company and its subsidiaries.” The full process of filing for insolvency in this manner allows them time to continue operating while renegotiating debt with creditors, and seeking a new financial path forward, with assistance and supervision from judicial administrators.

This whole crisis started in February when Nacon parent and majority stakeholder Bigben Interactive defaulted on a €43 million bond repayment, which sent shockwaves through its subsidiary. In the meantime, Nacon has postponed its Nacon Connect showcase, which had been due in early March.

That stream would no doubt have heavily featured Nacon games coming out in March and April, though Nacon has been able to push on and release these games. Unfortunately, they might not move the needle much at this time. Two games launching out of Early Access this month, GreedFall: The Dying World has a decidedly mixed reaction both from critics and players, and while hack ‘n’ slash RPG Dragonkin: The Banished has a better player response but only a smattering of critic reviews. The next big launch is Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss on 16th April.

We sincerely hope that Nacon is able to find a successful path ahead, making up a significant part of the French game development industry and providing much needed variety of games that they offer.

Source: Nacon



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/25/four-nacon-owned-studios-file-for-insolvency-spiders-cyanide-kylotonn-and-nacon-tech/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Playing With History launches soon – Here’s how to get a copy
item.content:encoded:
In case you missed it, last year Ade and Jim ran a successful crowdfunding campaign for their first book, Playing With History, based on the popular feature series we’ve been hosting on TSA for nearly a decade now, putting historical video games under the spotlight.

From cinematic samurai epics like Ghost of Yōtei to world-conquering strategy games such as the Total War series, they explore a wide spectrum of titles, including AAA blockbusters, indie gems and everything in between. Playing With History is set to feature over 200 pages and will be available both physically and digitally.

If you haven’t reserved a copy of the book already, you have just a few days left to make a late pledge via Kickstarter, whether you want a digital, paperback, or hardcover edition. Playing With History will start shipping worldwide in late May 2026.

Since the crowdfunding campaign, the project has grown in its scope and ambition. Instead of adapting a collection of existing articles for print, Ade and Jim have filled the book’s pages with new, original deep dive features and interviews. You can preview some of those interviews here:

  • Nioh 3 Interview – Exploring the hit series’ historical fantasy fusion
  • From Sports Sims to Strategy Kings: The Total War Story
  • Skull and Bones – How Ubisoft found fun in the Golden Age of Piracy

We also have a Playing With History preview that is free to download and read here.

Playing With History Archive

  • The Mad Mythology Behind Assassin’s Creed Odyssey
  • How Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown is steeped in Persian mythology
  • Wo Long and why games love The Yellow Turban Rebellion
  • Battlefield V And The Women Soldiers Of WW2
  • Why are Viking video games so popular right now?
  • Asssassin’s Creed Valhalla DLC – what was The Siege of Paris?
  • The missing history of Assassin’s Creed Valhalla
  • Ghost of Tsushima and the real world history behind Sony’s latest PS4 exclusive
  • Ubisoft on history and creating new heroes in For Honor
  • Kratos vs. Norse Mythology
  • Wolfenstein And The Third Reich’s Paranormal Activity
  • What’s Next for God of War?
  • Dissecting The Setting Of Hellblade: Senua’s Sacrifice
  • Just How Accurate Was Far Cry Primal?
  • Dissecting Assassin’s Creed Origins’ Historical Background
  • Fossil Hunting With Reptoid Games
  • Investigating Assassin’s Creed Odyssey
  • Seeking the Shinobi secrets of Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
  • Jurassic World Evolution And Tyrannosaurus Rex
  • The Impressionist Art Of 11-11: Memories Retold
  • The Unbelievable Weapons Missing From Battlefield 1
  • 11-11 Memories Retold And The Human Cost Of WWI
  • Playing With History – Days Gone And Zombie History
  • Radio Commander and the challenges of historical authenticity
  • Cutting into Story of a Gladiator
  • Illuminating the insane game world of Inkulinati
  • How HOOD: Outlaws and Legends is playing with history and fantasy
  • Dealing With The Burden Of Command
  • The forbidden gay love of Sundown: A Dunkirk Story
  • How the past helped build Lamplight City


https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/25/playing-with-history-book-kickstarter/
